simulation_result.question: Will Apple announce visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.8333 (counted n=2); simulation-conditional 0.0 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.0 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.316: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.316] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: Apple's reported abandonment of Vision Pro platform and shift to smart glasses, combined with lack of new hardware plans, makes a major visionOS announcement unlikely. However, WWDC tradition and the need to support existing Vision Pro users could still compel a minor update or a nominal major versi

apple_os_naming_convention.claim: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.

provenance.definition: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Apple announced visionOS 26 at WWDC 2025, using year-based naming for the first time.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

apple_vision_pro_sales_decline.claim: Apple Vision Pro sales have been low and the platform is considered abandoned by Apple internally.

provenance.definition: Apple Vision Pro sales have been low and the platform is considered abandoned by Apple internally.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: MacRumors reported that the M5 Vision Pro refresh failed to revive interest, with total sales only approximately 1.2 million units.
cases.basis_quote: The M5 refresh of Apple Vision Pro failed to revive consumer interest, with total sales reaching only approximately 1.2 million units.

cases.description: Apple stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to Siri, cancelled Vision Air, and shifted focus to AI smart glasses.
cases.basis_quote: Apple has reportedly stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled the Vision Air project, and shifted focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Apple announces visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026

provenance.definition: A public announcement by Apple of a new major version of visionOS that succeeds visionOS 26, occurring between May 6, 2026 and June 12, 2026, either during the WWDC 2026 keynote or via an official Apple Newsroom press release.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: The announcement must explicitly name a new major version (e.g., visionOS 27, visionOS 3) intended to replace visionOS 26.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Minor updates like visionOS 26.5 or 26.6 are excluded.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.
Apple today announced visionOS 2, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

latent_beliefs: Annual OS announcements are a key marketing event
Not announcing would be seen as a failure

expectations from analysts and media
public_facts: (reported) [2025-06-09] Apple announced visionOS 26 at WWDC 2025. — “Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.”
(reported) [2024-06-10] Apple announced visionOS 2 at WWDC 2024. — “Apple today announced visionOS 2, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.”

(confirmed) [2025-06-09] Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'. — “Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.”

(confirmed) [2026-04-29] Apple Vision Pro sales have been low and the platform is considered abandoned by Apple internally. — “The M5 refresh of Apple Vision Pro failed to revive consumer interest, with total sales reaching only approximately 1.2 million units.”

(confirmed) [2025-06-09] Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June. — “Apple today announced iOS 19, iPadOS 19, macOS 16, watchOS 12, and visionOS 26.”
commitments: Support the Vision Pro ecosystem narrative

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists
numeric predicates need a bounded mechanism in question units
synthetic YES→1 / NO→0 through the live path

aliases_yes: visionos_major_announcement event occurs on or before 2026-0

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: We lack clarity on how internal factions and developer sentiment would react to either choice.
deliberations.note: Internal factions pushing to move on from visionOS are a real concern, but abandoning the annual update now would confirm their narrative and alienate the small but dedicated developer base. Media scr

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

provenance.quantity: Apple announces visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

include_in_keynote.description: Include visionOS major version in WWDC keynote script.

issue_press_release.description: Publish Apple Newsroom press release announcing visionOS major version.

omit_from_keynote.description: Do not include visionOS major version in keynote script.

novel_cast_vote.description: Greg Joswiak casts a vote in an institutional process, recording the action and potentially setting a state.
